# Image Description

The image is a black-and-white construction framing diagram showing a **wall section** with labeled structural components, references to building code sections, and small detail sketches. It appears to illustrate **wood-frame wall construction**, including top and bottom plates, studs, headers, floor framing, foundation cripple wall framing, anchor bolts, fireblocking around a pipe, and corner/partition post details.

## Main Framing Section

The central drawing is a vertical wall framing section extending from the **top plate** down to the **foundation cripple wall** and **foundation wall studs** below.

### Top of Wall

At the top horizontal member, the following labels appear:

- **SINGLE OR DOUBLE TOP PLATE**
- **CUT PLATE TIED WITH 16 GAGE STEEL STRAP SEE SECTION R602.6.1**
- **STAGGER JOINTS 24 IN. OR USE SPLICE PLATES SEE SECTION R602.3.2**

These notes point to the top plate assembly running horizontally across the wall.

### Pipe and Fireblocking

Near the left side of the wall cavity, a vertical pipe is shown passing downward through the wall framing. A note points to the area around it:

- **FIREBLOCK AROUND PIPE**

### Wall Studs

Along the wall are multiple vertical studs. A note on the left identifies them as:

- **WALL STUDS SEE SECTION R602.3**

### Opening Framing

In the middle of the wall is a framed opening with a header at the top and shorter studs beneath and beside it.

Labels include:

- **FULL-HEIGHT STUDS ADJACENT TO HEADER SEE SECTION R602.7.5**
- **HEADER SEE TABLES R602.7(1) AND R602.7(2)**
- **JACK STUDS OR TRIMMERS**

The header spans horizontally across the opening. Full-height studs are shown beside it, and jack studs/trimmers support the header.

### Bottom Plate and Floor Assembly

At the base of the wall framing, the following labels identify the lower framing members:

- **BOTTOM PLATE**
- **SUBFLOOR**
- **SILL PLATE**

Below the subfloor, floor framing is shown with joists and blocking. Labels include:

- **SOLID BLOCKING**
- **FLOOR JOISTS**

### Foundation Cripple Wall and Foundation Wall Studs

Below the floor framing is a short framed wall section. Notes identify it as:

- **FOUNDATION CRIPPLE WALL—SEE SECTION R602.9**
- **FOUNDATION WALL STUDS**

### Anchor Bolts

Several anchor bolts are shown fastening framing to the foundation. A note reads:

- **ANCHOR BOLTS EMBEDDED IN FOUNDATION 6 FT. O.C. MAX.**

## Diagonal Brace Detail

At the lower left is a small detail sketch showing diagonal bracing let into studs. The note reads:

- **1 IN. BY 4 IN. DIAGONAL BRACE LET INTO STUDS**

Below that is a longer instruction paragraph:

- **APPLY APPROVED SHEATHING OR BRACE EXTERIOR WALLS WITH 1 IN. BY 4 IN. BRACES LET INTO STUDS AND PLATES AND EXTENDING FROM BOTTOM PLATE TO TOP PLATE, OR OTHER APPROVED METAL STRAP DEVICES INSTALLED IN ACCORDANCE WITH THE MANUFACTURER'S SPECIFICATIONS. SEE SECTION R602.10.**

## Corner and Partition Posts

At the lower right are three small framing detail sketches under the heading:

- **CORNER AND PARTITION POSTS**

Below these sketches is a note:

- **NOTE: A THIRD STUD AND/OR PARTITION INTERSECTION BACKING STUDS SHALL BE PERMITTED TO BE OMITTED THROUGH THE USE OF WOOD BACKUP CLEATS, METAL DRYWALL CLIPS OR OTHER APPROVED DEVICES THAT WILL SERVE AS ADEQUATE BACKING FOR THE FACING MATERIALS.**

# Image Description

The image is a black-and-white technical line drawing of a **wood stud wall framing section** shown in a 3D/isometric view. It illustrates vertical **studs** between a bottom plate and double **top plates**, with examples of **bored holes** and **notches** in studs, along with construction rules and dimensional limits.

## Main Components Labeled

- **TOP PLATES**
- **STUD**

## Visible Notes and Text

### Top of wall
- **TOP PLATES**

This label points to the double horizontal members at the top of the wall.

### Right side of wall
- **STUD**

This label points to one of the vertical framing members.

### Left-middle note about bored holes
- **BORED HOLE MAX. DIAMETER 40 PERCENT OF STUD DEPTH**

This note points to a circular bored hole through a stud.

### Left-side edge clearance note
- **5/8 IN. MIN. TO EDGE**

This note appears near the bored hole on the left stud, indicating minimum edge distance.

### Lower-left notch limitation
- **NOTCH MUST NOT EXCEED 25 PERCENT OF STUD DEPTH**

This note points to a rectangular notch cut into a stud.

### Lower-left note about hole/notch location
- **BORED HOLES SHALL NOT BE LOCATED IN THE SAME CROSS SECTION OF CUT OR NOTCH IN STUD**

This note points toward the area where a stud contains both a notch and a hole at different heights, emphasizing they must not align in the same cross section.

### Right-side edge clearance note
- **5/8 IN. MIN. TO EDGE**

This note appears again near another bored hole in a stud on the right side.

### Lower-right note about larger holes and doubled studs
- **IF HOLE IS BETWEEN 40 PERCENT AND 60 PERCENT OF STUD DEPTH, THEN STUD MUST BE DOUBLE AND NO MORE THAN TWO SUCCESSIVE STUDS ARE DOUBLED AND SO BORED**

This note points to a doubled stud assembly with a larger bored hole.

## What the Drawing Shows

- A framed wall section with:
  - a **bottom plate**
  - multiple vertical **studs**
  - **double top plates**
- Several studs include:
  - **small bored holes**
  - **notches**
  - one example of a **doubled stud** with a larger bored hole
- Arrows and leader lines connect each rule to the relevant stud condition.

## Interpreted Layout

From left to right, the wall includes examples of:
1. A stud with a **bored hole** meeting the rule:
   - **BORED HOLE MAX. DIAMETER 40 PERCENT OF STUD DEPTH**
   - **5/8 IN. MIN. TO EDGE**
2. A stud with a **notch** meeting the rule:
   - **NOTCH MUST NOT EXCEED 25 PERCENT OF STUD DEPTH**
3. A nearby condition illustrating:
   - **BORED HOLES SHALL NOT BE LOCATED IN THE SAME CROSS SECTION OF CUT OR NOTCH IN STUD**
4. A stud on the right with another bored hole and:
   - **5/8 IN. MIN. TO EDGE**
5. A **doubled stud** condition for a larger hole:
   - **IF HOLE IS BETWEEN 40 PERCENT AND 60 PERCENT OF STUD DEPTH, THEN STUD MUST BE DOUBLE AND NO MORE THAN TWO SUCCESSIVE STUDS ARE DOUBLED AND SO BORED**

|---|---|
| Top member label | **TOP PLATES** |

# Image Description

The image is a black-and-white technical diagram containing **two side-by-side plan illustrations** related to braced wall layouts in a building. Each diagram includes labeled wall lines, spacing dimensions, notes, and several **4' MAX** callouts.

---

## Overall Layout

- **Left diagram title:** **TYPICAL BRACED WALL PLAN**
- **Right diagram title:** **TYPICAL UPPER FLOOR BRACED WALL PLAN**
- A note appears at the bottom left of the image:
  - **For SI: 1 foot = 304.8 mm.**

The drawings use:
- solid black lines for walls,
- black filled circles at key intersections/endpoints,
- dashed and dash-dot reference lines,
- arrows and dimension lines,
- multiple labels for **BWL** wall lines and spacing between them.

---

# Left Diagram: Typical Braced Wall Plan

## Title
**TYPICAL BRACED WALL PLAN**

## Top Labels and Dimensions

Across the top of the left diagram are three vertical reference wall lines labeled:

- **BWL A**
- **BWL B**
- **BWL C**

Between these are horizontal dimension labels:

- **SPACING BETWEEN BWL A-B**
- **SPACING BETWEEN BWL B-C**

## Left Side Vertical Labels

Along the far left side are vertical labels:

- **BWL 1**
- **SPACING BETWEEN BWL 1-3**
- **BWL 3**

These indicate the vertical braced wall lines and spacing.

## Right Side Vertical Labels

Along the right side of the left diagram are vertical labels:

- **BWL 1**
- **SPACING BETWEEN BWL 1-2**
- **BWL 2**
- **SPACING BETWEEN BWL 2-4**
- **BWL 4**

## Building/Wall Shape

The floor plan outline is irregular.

### Upper portion
- Starts at the upper left with a black dot on **BWL A**.
- Extends rightward, then steps upward into a rectangular bump-out near the top center-left.
- Continues horizontally across to the right edge ending at a black dot on **BWL C**.

### Left side
- The left wall descends vertically from the upper left.
- Midway down, there is an angled inward/outward jog creating a faceted projection/recess.
- The wall continues downward to the lower left corner at a black dot.

### Bottom portion
- From the lower left, the wall runs rightward.
- There is a small rectangular upward bump near the lower left-center.
- The wall continues rightward to a vertical drop near the lower center-right.
- From there, the wall drops downward to the lowest point at a black dot.
- Then it runs rightward to the lower right corner at a black dot.

### Right side
- The right exterior wall is a straight vertical line from the top right black dot down to the lower right black dot.
- A black dot appears at the midpoint where an interior horizontal line meets the right wall.

## Interior Lines and Features

- A vertical dash-dot reference line runs through **BWL B** from top to bottom.
- Near the upper center-right, a vertical interior wall extends downward from the top horizontal wall.
- At mid-height, a horizontal interior wall extends from near the center toward the right exterior wall, ending at the midpoint black dot on the right wall.
- Near the lower center, a short vertical interior wall rises upward from the lower horizontal wall.

## Text and Callouts

Visible text inside the left diagram includes multiple instances of:

- **4' MAX**
- **4' MAX**
- **4' MAX**
- **4' MAX**
- **4' MAX**
- **4' MAX**

These are placed:
- vertically near the left upper interior,
- vertically near the top stepped projection,
- horizontally near the left middle recess,
- horizontally near the center interior,
- horizontally near the lower center-right,
- vertically below the small lower bump.

---

# Right Diagram: Typical Upper Floor Braced Wall Plan

## Title
**TYPICAL UPPER FLOOR BRACED WALL PLAN**

## Top Labels and Dimensions

Across the top of the right diagram are three vertical reference wall lines labeled:

- **BWL A**
- **BWL B**
- **BWL C**

Between them are horizontal dimension labels:

- **SPACING BETWEEN BWL A-B**
- **SPACING BETWEEN BWL B-C**

## Left Side Vertical Labels

Along the left side are vertical labels:

- **BWL 1**
- **SPACING BETWEEN END OF BUILDING AND BWL 1**
- **END OF BUILDING**

## Building Outline

This plan is more rectangular than the left one.

### Top edge
- Begins at a black dot on the left at **BWL A**.
- Runs horizontally rightward with a small step upward near the left side.
- Continues straight across through a black dot at **BWL B** and ends at a black dot at **BWL C**.

### Left edge
- A straight vertical exterior wall runs downward from the top left black dot to the bottom left black dot.

### Bottom edge
- A straight horizontal wall runs from the bottom left black dot to the bottom right black dot.
- There is a black dot at the bottom center aligned with **BWL B**.

### Right edge
- A straight vertical exterior wall runs from the top right black dot to the bottom right black dot.

## Interior and Dashed Elements

- A dashed vertical reference line runs through **BWL B**.
- Near the upper center, a vertical interior wall extends downward from the top wall.
- Near the lower center, another vertical interior wall extends upward from the bottom wall.
- A dashed rectangular outline appears below the bottom wall near the right side, extending downward outside the main rectangle.
- A dashed rectangular outline also appears near the upper left-center above the top wall step.

## Large Interior Note Area

Inside the main rectangle is a large sloped/outlined shape with angled sides, containing the note:

- **NO BRACED WALL LINE; ROOF AND FLOOR BEAR AT SAME ELEVATION**

This note is centered within the shape.

## Additional Notes

Near the lower left of the right diagram is a note:

- **NOTE: IN THE ABSENCE OF A BRACED WALL LINE, BWL A, B, C SHALL END AT EXTERIOR WALL**

## Text and Callouts

Visible **4' MAX** labels in the right diagram include:

- **4' MAX** vertically near the left upper interior
- **4' MAX** horizontally near the upper center-right
- **4' MAX** horizontally near the lower center-right, shown with a leading dash as:
  - **-4' MAX**

# Image Description

The image is a black-and-white technical diagram showing an irregular building wall outline with labeled **braced wall lines**, a **projected corner**, and a note about how diagonal wall segments are treated.

## Overall Layout

- The main figure is a polygonal wall perimeter, mostly rectangular, with:
  - a **diagonal/chamfered corner** at the upper left,
  - a **small inward jog/notch** along the upper wall near the center-right,
  - otherwise straight wall segments around the perimeter.
- The wall outline is drawn with double parallel lines, indicating wall thickness.
- Dimension arrows and dashed projection lines are used to indicate braced wall lines and projected bracing length.

## Visible Text

### Top Labels
- **PROJECTED CORNER**
- **BRACED WALL LINE 1**
- **PROJECTED LENGTH OF BRACING**

### Left Vertical Label
- **BRACED WALL LINE 2**

### Diagonal Segment Label
- **8' MAX. TYP.**

### Central Note
- **NOTE: IF THE DIAGONAL WALL IS GREATER THAN 8 FEET LONG, THEN IT MUST BE TREATED AS A SEPARATE BRACED WALL LINE**

## Diagram Details

### Upper Left Corner
- The upper-left corner is labeled **PROJECTED CORNER**.
- A leader arrow points to a small circled point at the corner projection.
- Dashed vertical and horizontal projection lines extend from this projected corner.

### Braced Wall Line 1
- At the top of the image, a long horizontal dimension line with arrows at both ends is labeled:
  - **BRACED WALL LINE 1**
- This line spans across the top width of the wall configuration.

### Projected Length of Bracing
- Below the top braced wall line label is another horizontal dashed reference line labeled:
  - **PROJECTED LENGTH OF BRACING**
- Small dimension arrows appear along this line near both ends.

### Braced Wall Line 2
- On the left side of the image, a vertical dimension line with arrows at top and bottom is labeled:
  - **BRACED WALL LINE 2**
- It runs alongside the left wall height.

### Diagonal Wall Segment
- Near the upper-left portion of the wall, the corner is cut at an angle.
- A dimension annotation aligned with this diagonal segment reads:
  - **8' MAX. TYP.**
- Arrows indicate the length of the diagonal wall segment.

### Top Wall Shape
- Starting from the upper-left diagonal, the top wall runs horizontally.
- Near the middle-right, the top wall steps inward and then back outward, forming a shallow notch/jog.
- The wall then continues horizontally to the upper-right corner.

### Main Note
Centered inside the wall outline is the note:

- **NOTE: IF THE DIAGONAL WALL IS GREATER THAN 8 FEET LONG, THEN IT MUST BE TREATED AS A SEPARATE BRACED WALL LINE**

| Location | Text |
|---|---|
| Upper left | PROJECTED CORNER |

It emphasizes that a **diagonal wall segment** may be included in the projected bracing length only up to **8 feet maximum typical**, and if the diagonal wall exceeds **8 feet**, it must be considered **a separate braced wall line**.
